---
title: "Cite ImmPrint"
---

If ImmPrint contributes to your work, please cite the **specific version** you used
so your analysis stays reproducible.

## DOI

A versioned DOI is minted through [Zenodo](https://zenodo.org/) for each release.

::: callout-note
The first archived release will carry a Zenodo DOI, and this page will then list
both the concept DOI (which always resolves to the latest version) and the
per-version DOI. Until then, cite the version and URL below.
:::

## Citation

> McLean, A. K. (2026). *ImmPrint: a curated, citation-backed collection of immune
> signalling gene sets* (Version 0.2.0-alpha) [Data set].
> <https://akmclz.github.io/immprint>

## BibTeX

```bibtex
@misc{immprint,
  author       = {McLean, Anthony K.},
  title        = {{ImmPrint: a curated, citation-backed collection of immune
                  signalling gene sets}},
  year         = {2026},
  version      = {0.2.0-alpha},
  howpublished = {\url{https://akmclz.github.io/immprint}},
  note         = {Accessed: YYYY-MM-DD}
}
```

A machine-readable [`CITATION.cff`](https://github.com/akmclz/immprint/blob/main/CITATION.cff)
is included in the repository; GitHub renders a "Cite this repository" button from
it.

## Citing the collection versus the software

- Cite **ImmPrint** (this page) for the gene set definitions and data.
- Cite the **`immprintr`** package separately if you want to credit the software,
  using `citation("immprintr")` once it is installed.
